title: Calibration Protocol for Plate Reader Abs600 with Microsphere Particles
author: Jacob Beal
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgy6jxze

steps.title: Obtain the tube labeled “Silica Beads”...
steps.instruction: Obtain the tube labeled “Silica Beads” from the Measurement Kit and vortex vigorously for 30 seconds. 300µl Silica beads Contributed by users Microspheres should NOT be stored at 0°C or below , as freezing affects the properties of the microspheres. If you believe your microspheres may have been frozen, please contact the iGEM Measurement Committee for a replacement (measurement@igem.org).

steps.title: Immediately pipet 100 μL microspheres into a 1
steps.instruction: Immediately pipet 100 μL microspheres into a 1.5 mL eppendorf tube

steps.title: Add 900 μL of ddH 2...
steps.instruction: Add 900 μL of ddH 2 O​ to the microspheres ddH20 Contributed by users

steps.title: Vortex well
steps.instruction: Vortex well. This is your Microsphere Stock Solution

steps.title: Accurate pipetting is essential
steps.instruction: Accurate pipetting is essential. Serial dilutions will be performed across columns 1-11. Column 12 must contain ddH​ 2 O only . Initially you will setup the plate with the microsphere stock solution in column 1 and an equal volume of 1x ddH 2 O​ in columns 2 to 12. You will perform a serial dilution by consecutively transferring 100 μl from column to column with good mixing.

steps.title: Add 100 μl of ddH​ 2...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μl of ddH​ 2 O​ ​ into wells A2, B2, C2, D2....A12, B12, C12, D12

steps.title: Vortex the tube containing the stock...
steps.instruction: Vortex the tube containing the stock solution of microspheres vigorously for 10 seconds

steps.title: Immediately add 200 μl​ ​of microspheres stock​ ​solution into A1
steps.instruction: Immediately add 200 μl​ ​of microspheres stock​ ​solution into A1

steps.title: Transfer 100 μl of microsphere stock...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 μl of microsphere stock solution from A1 into A2

steps.title: Mix A2 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A2 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A3

steps.title: Mix A3 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A3 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A4

steps.title: Mix A4 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A4 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A5

steps.title: Mix A5 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A5 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A6

steps.title: Mix A6 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A6 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A7

steps.title: Mix A7 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A7 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A8

steps.title: Mix A8 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A8 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A9

steps.title: Mix A9 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A9 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A10

steps.title: Mix A10 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A10 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into A11

steps.title: Mix A11 by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix A11 by pipetting up and down 3x and transfer 100 μl into ​liquid waste Take care not to continue serial dilution into column 12

steps.title: Repeat dilution series for rows B, C, D
steps.instruction: Repeat dilution series for rows B, C, D

steps.title: IMPORTANT
steps.instruction: IMPORTANT! ​ Re-Mix (pipette up and down) each row of your plate immediately before putting in the plate reader! (This is important because the beads begin to settle to the bottom of the wells within about 10 minutes, which will affect the measurements.) Take care to mix gently and avoid creating bubbles on the surface of the liquid

steps.title: Measure OD 600 ​ of all samples in instrument
steps.instruction: Measure OD 600 ​ of all samples in instrument. Disable any path length correction setting on your instrument, if it has one. If you will be using your data in conjuction with measurements from the Fluorescence standard curve protocol, make sure you use the same instrument settings for both protocols.

steps.title: Record the data in your notebook
steps.instruction: Record the data in your notebook. Please note your standard curve should still work well even if a few of your measurements are saturing the instrument

steps.title: Import data into this Excel sheet
steps.instruction: Import data into this Excel sheet: iGEM Data Analysis Template - Particle Standard Curve - v1.xlsx

steps.title: You have now completed this calibration protocol
steps.instruction: You have now completed this calibration protocol
